Adding to that is a crisp IPS panel that delivers Quad HD+ resolution. The configuration with the discrete RTX 3050 will offer a variable refresh rate of up to 144Hz, but the standard Gram 16 with integrated Intel Iris Xe graphics comes with a standard 60Hz IPS panel. You also get a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello facial recognition support, and all of these are things you get by default.

Finally, there's the design, with the first highlight being the fact that this 16-inch laptop starts at just 2.62 pounds for the configuration with integrated graphics. The dedicated GPU will factor into the weight, bringing the total weight of the LG Gram 16 to 2.86 pounds from 2.62 pounds. There are slightly lighter 16-inch laptops out there, but this one makes up for it in other ways, especially performance. As for ports, you get two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader, so it's a solid setup, all things considered.

Source: Lenovo
Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 (16-inch, 2023)
Best budget laptop

A solid experience on the cheap

$763 $850 Save $87

The Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 is a relatively basic laptop, but it covers the basics with solid performance and it has a 16-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio. Plus, since it's a convertible, it supports touch and pen input, too.

Pros
  • Modern AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors
  • Touchscreen with pen support and a 16:10 aspect ratio
  • 1080p webcam
Cons
  • Fairly heavy
  • Display doesn't have the best colors
$763 at Amazon $970 at Lenovo $699 at Best Buy

Not everyone wants to spend thousands of dollars on a laptop, but the 16-inch form factor tends to be reserved for more premium products. Thankfully, the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 is a great exception that does enough to give you a good experience for day-to-day use.

The base model of the IdeaPad Flex 5 comes with an AMD Ryzen 5 7530U, a solid processor with 6 cores and 12 threads that should be more than enough for web browsing, watching movies, and writing up documents and presentations. It also has 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD at minimum. There are upgrade options here if you can afford them, though.

Having a 16-inch display in this price range is fairly rare, but it's great to see one with a 16:10 aspect ratio and Full HD+ resolution, even if the colors won't look amazing here. Plus, since this is a convertible, you can use touch input and even a pen (included in some models) to interact with it more naturally. To top it off, there's even a 1080p webcam, which lacks Windows Hello support, but you can use a fingerprint reader for that instead.

Design is one area that tends to get sacrifices in budget laptops, and here, it kind of shows. This one uses a mix of aluminum and plastic for the build, and it makes for a heavy laptop, starting at 4.63 pounds. Again, not unexpected for something in this price range. It does come in two color options, though both are fairly subdued. As for ports, one USB-C connection, two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader make for a pretty good experience overall.

For a regular price starting at $700, the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 is a great laptop with a versatile form factor for a very reasonable price. If you want to save money, this is the way to go.

Best 16-inch laptops in 2023: Bottom line

These options cover a lot of use cases and there's something for everyone here. If you want the best overall experience, the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is hard to beat. It has fast processors, a fantastic display, a premium design, and even a solid supply of ports that beats many competitors in the same space. It's a huge leap forward for the series, too, and it's good to see Samsung invest heavily in making a premium device.

Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best overall
$1250 $1450 Save $200

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a premium aluminum laptop with 13th-generation Intel Core processors and a stunning AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate. It also has a solid selection of ports.

$1250 at Samsung $1450 at Best Buy (16 inches) $1250 at Amazon (16 inches)

If you can't afford something that expensive, you can still have a great time with the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5. It doesn't have the amazing display or the same level of performance, but for day-to-day use at home or at school, it's still great. It has a large screen, complete with touch support, and modern AMD processors that should deliver strong performance overall.
item.content:encoded:
For many years, 15-inch laptops were some of the most popular on the market since they offer a nice balance of portability and screen real estate. But in recent years, you may have noticed a trend, with more and more 16-inch laptops emerging and, in many cases, replacing 15-inch laptops. Many of the best laptops you can buy today have 16-inch screens, and very few have 15-inch displays.

The reason for that is simple — we're not just making screens bigger. In most cases, 16-inch laptops come in a 15-inch form factor, and that's because companies are moving away from the classic 16:9 aspect ratio that became the standard in the mid-2000s. Instead, we now have a taller 16:10 format, which is far better for productivity. The taller screen means you get more vertical space for lines of text, for video and audio tracks in a video editing interface, or for various tools in a photo editor like Photoshop.

There's essentially no downside to buying a 16-inch laptop compared to a 15-inch one these days, so, we're here to help you choose the best one for yourself. There's no shortage of options on the market at this point, and all of them are great, so let's dive right in.

  • Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
    Best overall
    $1250 at Samsung
  • HP Spectre x360 16 (2023)
    Best convertible
    $1150 at Best Buy
  • Apple MacBook Pro (2023)
    Best creator laptop
    $2299 at Amazon (16 inches)
  • HP EliteBook 860 G10
    Best business laptop
    $1132 at HP
  • Acer Chromebook 516 GE
    Best Chromebook
    $600 at Amazon
  • Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 8
    Best gaming laptop
    $1950 at Lenovo
  • LG Gram 16 (2023)
    Best lightweight laptop
    $1297 at Amazon
  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 (16-inch, 2023)
    Best budget laptop
    $699 at Best Buy
Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best overall

Fast performance, stunning display, and premium build

$1250 $1450 Save $200

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a premium aluminum laptop with 13th-generation Intel Core processors and a stunning AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate. It also has a solid selection of ports.

Pros
  • 13th-gen Intel Core processors deliver great performance
  • Beautiful Dynamic AMOLED 2X display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Good supply of ports
Cons
  • HDMI output limited to 4K at 30Hz
  • No Windows Hello facial recognition
$1250 at Samsung $1450 at Best Buy (16 inches) $1250 at Amazon (16 inches)

Samsung has been making some great laptops over the past few years, and in 2023, it became one of the best in the industry with the Galaxy Book 3 Pro.

In terms of performance, we're looking at 13th-generation Intel processors, up to a Core i7-1260P with 12 cores and 16 threads, which deliver great performance for day-to-day tasks. It's a mild, but welcome upgrade over the previous generation, and with 16GB of RAM and up to a 1TB SSD, you have all you need for a great experience.

The big upgrade is the display. The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is the first in the lineup to come in a 16-inch size thanks to switching to the 16:10 aspect ratio. The resolution is much sharper, too, at 2880x1800, and it even has a smooth 120Hz refresh rate now. It's simply better in every way. The webcam is the same 1080p sensor, and there's no facial recognition, but you can use Windows Hello with the fingerprint reader on the power button.

Another notable upgrade is the design, with Samsung switching to an aluminum chassis that feels much more premium and sturdy. It looks sleek and subdued in both Graphite and Beige colorways, too. Unfortunately, aluminum is much heavier than magnesium, so this laptop comes in at 3.44 pounds, even though it's still very thin. It's still portable, but significantly heavier than its predecessor. However, the port selection here is great, including two Thunderbolt 4 ports, USB Type-A, HDMI, a 3.5mm headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. The HDMI port is limited to

Previous Samsung Galaxy laptops were already great, but the changes made to the Galaxy Book 3 Pro make this one of the best laptops on the market.

HP Spectre x360 16 (2023)
Best convertible

A versatile form factor with powerful performance

$1150 $1650 Save $500

The 2023 HP Spectre x360 16 comes with powerful Intel processors and optional Intel Arc graphics for workloads that require more GPU power. It has a super-sharp 16-inch display, including an optional OLED panel, too.

Pros
  • Sharp 16-inch display with optional 4K OLED panel
  • 13th-gen Intel Core processors and Intel Arc graphics
  • 5MP webcam is one of the best in a laptop
Cons
  • Somewhat heavy
  • More performance than most people will need
$1700 at HP $1150 at Best Buy

Clamshell laptops are fine, but if you're like me, nothing beats the versatility of a convertible. If you're looking for a large one, the HP Spectre x360 16 is your best bet.

The latest model of the HP Spectre x360 16 comes with 13th-generation Intel processors, and you can choose between the 45W Core i7-13700H, with 14 cores and 20 threads, or the 28W Core i7-1360P, with 12 cores and 16 threads. Naturally, the former option is faster, but the P-series processor is combined with Intel Arc A370M graphics, which gives you a boost in GPU performance for creative work like photo or video editing. You can also get this laptop with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

HP hasn't changed the display from the previous iteration, but it didn't need to. This is a 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, and it comes in a very sharp 3072x1920 resolution by default. You can also upgrade to the 4K+ OLED panel, which is even sharper and has more vibrant colors and true blacks. Above that display also lives one of the best webcams you'll find on a laptop — a 5MP sensor with 1080p video and features like auto framing. It also supports Windows Hello facial recognition.

In terms of looks, the Spectre x360 16 comes in a unique dual-tone color scheme, mixing black with copper accents, or dark blue with light blue accents. Being a large convertible made of aluminum, it's a fairly heavy laptop, coming in at 4.45 pounds. Still, it's decently portable. As for ports, HP gives you two Thunderbolt 4 connections, one USB Type-A port, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. It's a pretty solid setup for a modern laptop.

The HP Spectre x360 16 can be a bit pricey, but it's a fantastic laptop in just about every way. We've reviewed the original iteration from late 2021, and aside from performance upgrades, not much has changed. It's still fantastic.

Apple MacBook Pro (2023)
Best creator laptop

Terrific performance and battery life

$2299 $2499 Save $200

The 2023 MacBook Pro builds on the 2021 model to deliver even more performance and efficiency while maintaining a top-tier display and build quality. It's one of the best laptops on the market, plain and simple.

Pros
  • Unrivaled performance and battery life thanks to the Apple M2 Pro and M2 Max chips
  • Mini-LED display with 120Hz refresh rate and up to 1,600 nits of brightness
  • Premium build quality with an all-aluminum chassis
Cons
  • It may look a bit boring to some
  • Not everyone wants to use macOS
  • It gets quite expensive
$2299 at Amazon (16 inches) $2299 at Best Buy (16 inches) $2499 at Apple (16 inches)

It's impossible to talk about the best laptops on the market without mentioning the MacBook Pro, especially if we're talking about 16-inch laptops. The latest model is a phenomenal machine for performance and battery life, making it easily the best laptop for creators.

Indeed, the Apple M2 Pro and M2 Max are top-of-the-line processors with 12 CPU cores (eight for performance, four for efficiency) and up to a 39-core GPU that delivers a balance of performance and battery life that you just can't get anywhere. The performance can rival the best laptops in the Windows space, but it's so efficient that battery life is in a whole different class compared to its competitors. You can also get up to 96GB of RAM and an 8TB SSD.

To go along with the powerful internals, there's also an absolutely fantastic screen. It's a 16.2-inch panel and it comes in a super-sharp 3456x2234 resolution, resulting in an aspect ratio slightly taller than 16:10. The screen has a 120Hz adaptive refresh rate that makes motion and animations look smoother. Plus, it has a Mini-LED display with hundreds of individual dimming zones, and it can reach a maximum brightness of 1,600 nits. The only thing you might not like is the notch in the middle of the screen, which is where you'll find the 1080p webcam that delivers very good image quality.

Design-wise, the MacBook Pro isn't particularly exciting, but it is premium. It's a solid aluminum laptop that comes in either Silver or Space Grey (dark silver), so it looks very subdued. Thanks to the powerful specs, it gets a bit heavy, starting a 4.7 pounds of weight. Finally, in terms of ports, the latest MacBook Pro models come with three Thunderbolt 4 ports, along with HDMI, a headphone jack, a full-size SD card reader, and a MagSafe connector for charging. It's worth noting that the Thunderbolt ports don't support things like external GPUs here, but otherwise, it's a solid setup.

This is undoubtedly one of the best laptops on the market, and while it's pricey, we couldn't not recommend it.

HP EliteBook 860 G10
Best business laptop

Subdued design and multiple configurations available

$1132 $2263 Save $1131

The HP EliteBook 860 G10 is a higher-end mainstream laptop with 13th-gen Intel Core processors and a subdued, sleek design. It also has a few configuration options available for its various components.

Pros
  • 13th-gen Intel U- or P-series processors
  • Subdued design with a lot of ports
  • 5MP webcam
Cons
  • Somewhat heavy
  • Expensive, especially for build-to-order configurations
$1132 at HP $1617 at Amazon $1639 at B&H

If you need a laptop for work and you want one with a 16-inch display to get more done, the EliteBook 860 G10 is a great option that checks all the boxes without being overly fancy.

On a performance level, you can choose 13th-generation Intel processors from either the P-series or the U-series. The P-series models go up to a Core i7-1370P with 14 cores and 20 threads, while the U-series maxes out with a Core i7-1365U with 10 cores and 12 threads. P-series processors can be faster, but the U series is better for battery life. Otherwise, you can go up to 64GB of RAM (slotted) and a 1TB SSD.

You also get a 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, and since this is a laptop focused on nailing the basics, the entry-level configuration comes in Full HD+ resolution and 250 nits of brightness. There are options for brighter panels, touch support, and HP Sure View Reflect, for keeping your data safe from prying eyes. However, you can also opt for a 2.8K OLED panel for a stunning visual experience. Above that display is also a 5MP webcam with optional Windows Hello support.

For a business laptop, the HP EliteBook 860 G10 is exactly what you'd expect. It comes in a simple silver chassis made of aluminum, and it's both premium and subdued, though not overly fancy. Starting at 3.8 pounds, it's not terribly light, either. However, it does have great connectivity, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and even an optional Smart Card reader. There's also optional 4G LTE or 5G support.

This kind of laptop doesn't change very often, so you can check out both our review of the EliteBook 865 G9 (the last-gen AMD version of this model) or the EliteBook 840 G9 (a smaller 14-inch model) to have an idea of what to expect. It's a solid business laptop that does everything you'd expect for a somewhat reasonable price.

Acer Chromebook 516 GE
Best Chromebook

Fast performance and a sharp display for ChromeOS

The Acer Chromebook 516 GE is marketed as a gaming laptop, but it's just a great laptop for anyone. With 12th-generation Intel Core processors and a sharp display, it's perfect for all kinds of users, plus it has some personality with the RGB keyboard.

Pros
  • Sharp Quad HD+ display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Fast 12th-gen Intel processors
  • RGB keyboard adds some personality
Cons
  • Windows laptops have newer processors now
  • No biometric authentication
$600 at Amazon $649 at Best Buy

There isn't a huge market for 16-inch Chromebooks out there, but that shouldn't take away from how good the Acer Chromebook 516 GE is. If you want the best ChromeOS laptop on the market, this should be on your mind, regardless of size.

Powering the Acer Chromebook 516 GE is a 12th-generation Intel Core i5-1240P processor with 12 cores and 16 threads. It's not the newest processor around, but it's still very fast, especially on ChromeOS. It also comes with 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD for storage. It may be advertised for gaming, but these are specs you'd find in any ultrabook, and that's actually a good thing since it makes this a great machine for anyone.

What makes it even better is the 16-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio and sharp Quad HD+ resolution. That makes for a great viewing experience, and it's excellent for getting work done. It even has a 120Hz refresh rate (where some of that gaming DNA comes in), so animations and motion look much smoother. There's also a 1080p webcam above the display, though there's no kind of biometric authentication here.

The Acer Chromebook 516 GE doesn't try to be too fancy in terms of looks, with a simple black design, but it does have an RGB keyboard, which adds a bit of flair if you're into that kind of thing. At 3.75 pounds, it's not exactly light, but it's reasonable for the size. Ports are pretty good, too, with two USB Type-C and one Type-A port, HDMI, RJ45 Ethernet, and a headphone jack.

As far as Chromebooks go, it doesn't get much better than the Chromebook 516 GE, and if you want something specifically of this size, you can't go wrong here.

Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 8
Best gaming laptop

Monster performance in a premium design

$2079 $2750 Save $671

The Lenovo Legion Pro 7i is a beastly gaming laptop with top-notch performance. It has incredibly powerful Intel processors and Nvidia graphics, along with a great display for gaming. It even has a premium metal design.

Pros
  • Up to a 24-core processor and Nvidia RTX 4090 graphics
  • Quad HD+ display with super-smooth 240Hz refresh rate
  • Lots of ports
Cons
  • No Windows Hello support
  • Heavy
  • Expensive
$1950 at Lenovo $2079 at B&H $1950 at Newegg

Gaming is one of the most popular hobbies in the world, and the 16-inch form factor is arguably ideal for it. If you want the best option in that field, the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i should definitely be on your radar.

Performance is one thing this laptop isn't missing, with up to a 24-core, 32-thread Intel Core i9-13900HX delivering absolutely outstanding performance for a laptop. That, combined with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, gives you all the performance you could want to run your games at high settings and high framerates. You can also configure it with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD to store all your games.

You're getting a great display, too — a 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio and super-sharp Quad HD+ resolution. This makes for a great panel for getting work done, too. And with the smooth 240Hz refresh rate, gaming is as enjoyable as it gets. Most games probably can't hit that maximum resolution and refresh rate, but for lighter titles, this is fantastic. There's also a 1080p webcam above the display, which is nice to see, though there's no Windows Hello support, not even through a fingerprint reader.

The Legion Pro 7i also impresses in terms of design, since it's a mostly metal chassis that looks and feels very premium. Combined with the high-end specs, this makes for a very heavy laptop, starting at 6.17 pounds, but that makes sense for something like this. And in terms of ports, we're looking at two USB-C ports (one with Thunderbolt 4), four USB Type-A ports, HDMI, RJ45 Ethernet, and a headphone jack, so there's no shortage of connectivity options.

There's a lot to love in the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i, and if you can afford it, it's one of the very best gaming laptops on the market.

LG Gram 16 (2023)
Best lightweight laptop

Fast performance in a portable package

$1297 $1700 Save $403

The LG Gram 16 is a lightweight and powerful 16-inch laptop that features a crisp display, solid battery life, and a very sleek design. Featuring Intel's 13th-generation CPUs, there's enough power for this to be your ultra portable work machine.

Pros
  • Incredibly sleek design
  • Sharp Quad HD+ display
  • Just 2.6 pounds
Cons
  • Lack of a touchscreen option
$1300 at Best Buy $1297 at Amazon $1300 at LG

Bigger laptops tend to be heavier (naturally), but if you want a machine that has a bigger screen, great performance, and is also super light, the LG Gram 16 is for you.

First off, in terms of performance, the LG Gram 16 comes with up to a 13th-generation Intel Core i7, with 12 cores and 16 threads handling day-to-day tasks without a hitch. You can choose between 16GB or 32GB of RAM, though, and you also get a 1TB SSD. There's no room for bad performance here, which helps explain the higher price tag. By default, the LG Gram 16 comes with integrated Intel Iris Xe graphics, but you have the option of upgrading to a model with a discrete Nvidia RTX 3050 for an extra boost to the graphics performance.

Adding to that is a crisp IPS panel that delivers Quad HD+ resolution. The configuration with the discrete RTX 3050 will offer a variable refresh rate of up to 144Hz, but the standard Gram 16 with integrated Intel Iris Xe graphics comes with a standard 60Hz IPS panel. You also get a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello facial recognition support, and all of these are things you get by default.

Finally, there's the design, with the first highlight being the fact that this 16-inch laptop starts at just 2.62 pounds for the configuration with integrated graphics. The dedicated GPU will factor into the weight, bringing the total weight of the LG Gram 16 to 2.86 pounds from 2.62 pounds. There are slightly lighter 16-inch laptops out there, but this one makes up for it in other ways, especially performance. As for ports, you get two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader, so it's a solid setup, all things considered.

Source: Lenovo
Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 (16-inch, 2023)
Best budget laptop

A solid experience on the cheap

$763 $850 Save $87

The Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 is a relatively basic laptop, but it covers the basics with solid performance and it has a 16-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio. Plus, since it's a convertible, it supports touch and pen input, too.

Pros
  • Modern AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors
  • Touchscreen with pen support and a 16:10 aspect ratio
  • 1080p webcam
Cons
  • Fairly heavy
  • Display doesn't have the best colors
$763 at Amazon $970 at Lenovo $699 at Best Buy

Not everyone wants to spend thousands of dollars on a laptop, but the 16-inch form factor tends to be reserved for more premium products. Thankfully, the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 is a great exception that does enough to give you a good experience for day-to-day use.

The base model of the IdeaPad Flex 5 comes with an AMD Ryzen 5 7530U, a solid processor with 6 cores and 12 threads that should be more than enough for web browsing, watching movies, and writing up documents and presentations. It also has 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD at minimum. There are upgrade options here if you can afford them, though.

Having a 16-inch display in this price range is fairly rare, but it's great to see one with a 16:10 aspect ratio and Full HD+ resolution, even if the colors won't look amazing here. Plus, since this is a convertible, you can use touch input and even a pen (included in some models) to interact with it more naturally. To top it off, there's even a 1080p webcam, which lacks Windows Hello support, but you can use a fingerprint reader for that instead.

Design is one area that tends to get sacrifices in budget laptops, and here, it kind of shows. This one uses a mix of aluminum and plastic for the build, and it makes for a heavy laptop, starting at 4.63 pounds. Again, not unexpected for something in this price range. It does come in two color options, though both are fairly subdued. As for ports, one USB-C connection, two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader make for a pretty good experience overall.

For a regular price starting at $700, the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 is a great laptop with a versatile form factor for a very reasonable price. If you want to save money, this is the way to go.

Best 16-inch laptops in 2023: Bottom line

These options cover a lot of use cases and there's something for everyone here. If you want the best overall experience, the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is hard to beat. It has fast processors, a fantastic display, a premium design, and even a solid supply of ports that beats many competitors in the same space. It's a huge leap forward for the series, too, and it's good to see Samsung invest heavily in making a premium device.

Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best overall
$1250 $1450 Save $200

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a premium aluminum laptop with 13th-generation Intel Core processors and a stunning AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate. It also has a solid selection of ports.

$1250 at Samsung $1450 at Best Buy (16 inches) $1250 at Amazon (16 inches)

If you can't afford something that expensive, you can still have a great time with the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5. It doesn't have the amazing display or the same level of performance, but for day-to-day use at home or at school, it's still great. It has a large screen, complete with touch support, and modern AMD processors that should deliver strong performance overall.

item.title: How to find the specs on your Windows 11 PC
item.itunes:title: How to find the specs on your Windows 11 PC
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • Find specs using the Windows 11 Settings app
  • Using the DirectX Diagnostic Tool
  • Using System Information
  • Using Windows Terminal

At one point or another, you might need to find the specifications — or specs — of your Windows 11 PC. It can be useful for troubleshooting if you're having issues, or maybe you just want to know if your laptop is compatible with a certain app or able to run a specific game. If you're more tech-savvy, of course, you probably already purchased your PC based on the specs, but that's not the case for everyone.

Thankfully, finding the specs after the fact isn't that hard, and there are a few ways you can do it. These methods vary in complexity and how much information they provide, so you can choose whatever fits your specific needs the best. We're here to help, and we're going to show you how you can find the specs of your Windows 11 PC using a few different methods.

Find specs using the Windows 11 Settings app

The first and easiest method to find the specs of your Windows 11 PC is to use the Settings app. This will give you a fairly basic rundown of the specs - including the processor and RAM, as well as information about your version of Windows. To do this, follow these steps:

  1. Open the Settings app (it should be in the Start menu).
  2. In the System section, scroll down and click About.
  3. Here you'll see your system specs, which include your processor model and the base clock speed, as well as the amount of RAM your computer has.
  4. Just below that, you'll also see your Windows specifications, so you can see what version and build of Windows you're running. You may be running the original release of Windows 11, or maybe you already have Windows 11 version 22H2, for example.

If you're seeing some software issues, they can be related to a specific version of Windows, so this is all useful to know. Still, this is a very limited set of specs, and you may want to know more. Let's explore some other methods.

Using the DirectX Diagnostic Tool

If you're mostly focused on specs related to gaming, or anything around the CPU, GPU, and audio devices, the DirectX Diagnostic Tool is a great way to find out more about your system's hardware and the features it supports. To open the DirectX Diagnostic Tool:

  1. Open the Start menu and type dxdiag, then hit Enter.
    • Alternatively, you can press the Windows key + R to open the Run window, then type dxdiag there.

  2. You'll be asked if you want to check whether your drivers are digitally signed. Click Yes.

This main page — the System tab — shows you basic system information, including your version and build of Windows, the BIOS version, and some information about your processor and RAM. The most interesting part of this, however, is the Display tab — there may actually be more than one if your PC has more than one GPU, as with many gaming laptops.

This shows you information including the name of your GPU, the amount of memory available to it, your driver version, and what DirectX features it supports. DirectX is essential for running games on your PC, so support for specific features may determine if some games can run well or not.

You also have the Audio tab with information about your audio drivers, and the Input tab lists connected input devices like a keyboard, mouse, and others.

Using System Information

If you want to get more detailed information about the specs on your Windows 11 PC, another great option is the aptly named System Information. This is an older tool that's been in Windows for many years.

The easiest way to open System Information is to simply open the Start menu and type System Information until it shows up in the search results. If that doesn't work, try this:

  1. Open the Start menu.
  2. Head into the All apps list.
  3. Scroll down and open Windows Tools.
  4. Click System Information.

The System Information window opens by default into a system summary, which includes information about your processor, including the base clock speed, the number of physical cores, and the logical processors (also known as threads). You can also find your RAM on this page.

If you want more information about other hardware inside your PC, expand the Components section on the side menu. There, you can find information about the display (including the GPU/graphics card), sound devices, storage, and more. Some sub-sections can also be expanded, so there's a lot you can find

Not every section here will be super useful to everyone, but this is one of the more detailed breakdowns you can get.

Using Windows Terminal

If you're someone who simply works better with command line-based tools, you can also use the Windows Terminal app to find your system specs. Windows Terminal is a console host that can run both Command Prompt and Windows PowerShell, and both of these tools let you find the specs of your PC, though with different commands and slightly different outputs. Command Prompt provides a slightly cleaner-looking output.

Using Windows PowerShell

  1. Open the Start menu and type Windows Terminal until it shows up in the search results and press Enter.
  2. Enter Get-ComputerInfo and press Type.
  3. To find information about a specific part of your PC, you can use Get-ComputerInfo Property followed by the name of the property you want to see. You can also use wildcards to see properties that start with similar names.
    2 Images

Using Command Prompt

  1. Open the Start menu and type Command prompt until it shows up in the search results then press Enter.
  2. If you're using Windows PowerShell, enter systeminfo and press Enter.

These methods don't include a ton of information about things like your GPU, but it's yet another way to find the specs on your PC.

And that's all you need to know to find the specs on a Windows 11 PC. Out of these methods, we'd say the DirectX Diagnostic Tool and System Information offer the most detailed information, but you might always prefer one of the other options. Once you know what you're looking for, any of these is a good way to get more information about your PC.
item.description:
Quick Links

  • Find specs using the Windows 11 Settings app
  • Using the DirectX Diagnostic Tool
  • Using System Information
  • Using Windows Terminal

At one point or another, you might need to find the specifications — or specs — of your Windows 11 PC. It can be useful for troubleshooting if you're having issues, or maybe you just want to know if your laptop is compatible with a certain app or able to run a specific game. If you're more tech-savvy, of course, you probably already purchased your PC based on the specs, but that's not the case for everyone.

Thankfully, finding the specs after the fact isn't that hard, and there are a few ways you can do it. These methods vary in complexity and how much information they provide, so you can choose whatever fits your specific needs the best. We're here to help, and we're going to show you how you can find the specs of your Windows 11 PC using a few different methods.

Find specs using the Windows 11 Settings app

The first and easiest method to find the specs of your Windows 11 PC is to use the Settings app. This will give you a fairly basic rundown of the specs - including the processor and RAM, as well as information about your version of Windows. To do this, follow these steps:

  1. Open the Settings app (it should be in the Start menu).
  2. In the System section, scroll down and click About.
  3. Here you'll see your system specs, which include your processor model and the base clock speed, as well as the amount of RAM your computer has.
  4. Just below that, you'll also see your Windows specifications, so you can see what version and build of Windows you're running. You may be running the original release of Windows 11, or maybe you already have Windows 11 version 22H2, for example.

If you're seeing some software issues, they can be related to a specific version of Windows, so this is all useful to know. Still, this is a very limited set of specs, and you may want to know more. Let's explore some other methods.

Using the DirectX Diagnostic Tool

If you're mostly focused on specs related to gaming, or anything around the CPU, GPU, and audio devices, the DirectX Diagnostic Tool is a great way to find out more about your system's hardware and the features it supports. To open the DirectX Diagnostic Tool:

  1. Open the Start menu and type dxdiag, then hit Enter.
    • Alternatively, you can press the Windows key + R to open the Run window, then type dxdiag there.

  2. You'll be asked if you want to check whether your drivers are digitally signed. Click Yes.

This main page — the System tab — shows you basic system information, including your version and build of Windows, the BIOS version, and some information about your processor and RAM. The most interesting part of this, however, is the Display tab — there may actually be more than one if your PC has more than one GPU, as with many gaming laptops.

This shows you information including the name of your GPU, the amount of memory available to it, your driver version, and what DirectX features it supports. DirectX is essential for running games on your PC, so support for specific features may determine if some games can run well or not.

You also have the Audio tab with information about your audio drivers, and the Input tab lists connected input devices like a keyboard, mouse, and others.

Using System Information

If you want to get more detailed information about the specs on your Windows 11 PC, another great option is the aptly named System Information. This is an older tool that's been in Windows for many years.

The easiest way to open System Information is to simply open the Start menu and type System Information until it shows up in the search results. If that doesn't work, try this:

  1. Open the Start menu.
  2. Head into the All apps list.
  3. Scroll down and open Windows Tools.
  4. Click System Information.

The System Information window opens by default into a system summary, which includes information about your processor, including the base clock speed, the number of physical cores, and the logical processors (also known as threads). You can also find your RAM on this page.

If you want more information about other hardware inside your PC, expand the Components section on the side menu. There, you can find information about the display (including the GPU/graphics card), sound devices, storage, and more. Some sub-sections can also be expanded, so there's a lot you can find

Not every section here will be super useful to everyone, but this is one of the more detailed breakdowns you can get.

Using Windows Terminal

If you're someone who simply works better with command line-based tools, you can also use the Windows Terminal app to find your system specs. Windows Terminal is a console host that can run both Command Prompt and Windows PowerShell, and both of these tools let you find the specs of your PC, though with different commands and slightly different outputs. Command Prompt provides a slightly cleaner-looking output.

Using Windows PowerShell

  1. Open the Start menu and type Windows Terminal until it shows up in the search results and press Enter.
  2. Enter Get-ComputerInfo and press Type.
  3. To find information about a specific part of your PC, you can use Get-ComputerInfo Property followed by the name of the property you want to see. You can also use wildcards to see properties that start with similar names.
    2 Images

Using Command Prompt

  1. Open the Start menu and type Command prompt until it shows up in the search results then press Enter.
  2. If you're using Windows PowerShell, enter systeminfo and press Enter.

These methods don't include a ton of information about things like your GPU, but it's yet another way to find the specs on your PC.

And that's all you need to know to find the specs on a Windows 11 PC. Out of these methods, we'd say the DirectX Diagnostic Tool and System Information offer the most detailed information, but you might always prefer one of the other options. Once you know what you're looking for, any of these is a good way to get more information about your PC.

  3. To find information about a specific part of your PC, you can use Get-ComputerInfo Property followed by the name of the property you want to see. You can also use wildcards to see properties that start with similar names.
    2 Images

Using Command Prompt

  1. Open the Start menu and type Command prompt until it shows up in the search results then press Enter.
  2. If you're using Windows PowerShell, enter systeminfo and press Enter.

These methods don't include a ton of information about things like your GPU, but it's yet another way to find the specs on your PC.

And that's all you need to know to find the specs on a Windows 11 PC. item.title: Discover Samsung event offers steep discounts on phones, monitors, smartwatches, and more
item.itunes:title: Discover Samsung event offers steep discounts on phones, monitors, smartwatches, and more
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • Foldable phones
  • Smartphones
  • Smartwatches
  • Monitors
  • Storage

It's that time of year again when Samsung brings the hottest deals on some of its best products. While the sales event officially starts next week on September 11, you're getting early access to some of the best deals on smartwatches, monitors, smartphones, and more. So if you've been eyeing that brand new Galaxy Z Fold 5, or just needed a new monitor, we've rounded up some of the best deals from the Discover Samsung Early Access event.

Foldable phones

Samsung has dominated the Android smartphone scene with its Galaxy Z and S series devices for some years now, utilizing the best processors, featuring impressive displays, and offering some of the best cameras on the market.

During the Discover Samsung event, the brand is offering some excellent incentives, with up to $1000 in credit just for trading in an eligible device. Furthermore, if you purchase Samsung accessories, you'll qualify to receive additional discounts as well.

  • Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5
    Save up to $1,000 with enhanced trade in and get a storage upgrade

    The Galaxy Z Fold 5 features a powerful Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 processor, 4,400mAh battery, and you can score up to $1,000 off with a trade-in during the Discover Samsung event.

    $1800 at Samsung
  • Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 5
    Save up to $600 with enhanced trade in

    The Galaxy Z Flip 5 features a powerful Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 processor, massive cover display, and you can score up to $600 off with a trade-in during the Discover Samsung event.

    $1000 at Samsung

Smartphones

  • Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra
    Save up to $880 with enhanced trade in

    The Galaxy S23 Ultra is one of the best phones on the market, featuring a Snapdragon 8 Gen 2, beautiful AMOLED display, incredible cameras, and more.

    $1200 at Samsung
  • Samsung Galaxy S23+
    Save up to $880 with enhanced trade in

    The Galaxy S23+ is also one of the best phones on the market, featuring a Snapdragon 8 Gen 2, beautiful AMOLED display, incredible cameras, and more.

    $1000 at Samsung
  • Samsung Galaxy S23
    Save up to $880 with enhanced trade in

    The Galaxy S23 is a great smartphone featuring a powerful Snapdragon 8 Gen 2, beautiful AMOLED display, incredible cameras, and more.

    $800 at Samsung

If you weren't looking to purchase one of Samsung's flagship devices, you'll be happy to know that the company is also offering up to $250 with trade in and also knocking $75 off its popular Galaxy A54, which is currently one of the best affordable smartphones available. The device has a beautiful display, great performance, a long-lasting battery, and gets reliable software updates. Best of all, it's currently on sale for a limited time.

Samsung Galaxy A54 5G
Save up to $325

The Samsung Galaxy A54 5G is a great mid-range phone. It offers a sleek design, a beautiful 120Hz display, plenty of power under the hood, and a triple camera setup on the back.

$450 at Samsung

Smartwatches

  • Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic
    Take $130 off Buds2 Pro

    Samsung's Galaxy Watch 6 Classic brings the largest screen in any Samsung smartwatch yet, and also features a new and improved rotating bezel, plus longer battery life.

    $400 at Samsung
  • Source: Samsung
    Samsung Galaxy Watch 6
    Take $130 off Buds2 Pro

    Samsung's Galaxy Watch 6 brings sleek style with plenty of power under the hood, along with a beautiful display. Furthermore, the watch provides excellent battery life.

    $300 at Samsung

In addition to producing excellent smartphones, the brand also manages to have an excellent line-up wearables. The Galaxy Watch 6 and Watch 6 Classic are some of the best smartwatches out right now and for good reasons. The devices deliver a great user experience, look sleek and modern, and also offer terrific battery life and features. Furthermore, both watches will continue to evolve thanks to continued software updates provided by Samsung over the next few years.

If you're looking for something a little more robust, you might want to check out Samsung's Galaxy Watch 5 Pro made from premium materials like Titanium and also featuring impressive battery life with its large 590mAh battery. While the watch will perform similar to its Galaxy Watch 6 siblings, there is one area where it excels and that's its custom GPX routing feature.

Samsung Galaxy Watch 5 Pro
Save up to $80
$400 $450 Save $50

The Galaxy Watch 5 Pro is aimed at fitness enthusiasts, packing a more durable design and a bigger battery than the standard Galaxy Watch 5.

$400 at Samsung

Monitors

There are a lot of great monitor options out there, but Samsung makes some of the best, which means, whenever you can find a great deal on one, you probably should pick one up. During the Discover Samsung event, the brand is knocking up to $1000 off its monitors, so now's going to be a great time to buy.

  • Samsung Odyssey Ark 55-inch 4K Quantum Curved Gaming Monitor

    Samsung's Odyssey Ark monitor is unlike any other. It packs a massive 55-inch Mini LED panel and can be rotated to a cool vertical cockpit mode

    $3000 at Samsung
  • Source: Samsung
    Samsung Odyssey OLED G9
    $1600 $1800 Save $200

    Samsung's Odyssey OLED G9 monitor offers fantastic colors and contrast and offers plenty of screen real estate with its massive 49-inch OLED panel.

    $1600 at Samsung
  • Samsung M8 32-inch 4K Smart Monitor
    $579 $700 Save $121

    The Samsung M8 is a compact 4K monitor that's quite versatile bolstered by its smart features that allow users to tune into their favorite streaming services.

    $579 at Samsung
  • 49” Odyssey Neo G9 Curved Smart Gaming Monitor (Save $800 / 37%)

Storage

Samsung T7 Touch Portable SSD

There are many ways to keep your data safe, but the Samsung T7 Touch SSD makes it easier since it allows you to unlock the data inside using the fingerprint sensor. It's not as fast some Thunderbolt SSDs, but it's still far from slow.

$160 at Samsung

These are just a handful of options when it comes to products on sale, but check back September 11 for the official start of the Discover Samsung event where the brand will offer weekly, daily, and even limited time flash sales on all its favorite products.

item.title: Best Lenovo Legion Go power banks in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best Lenovo Legion Go power banks in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The upcoming Lenovo Legion Go is a pretty exciting device. This Windows-powered handheld gaming device will let you run most PC games on a huge and bright 8.8-inch QHD+ screen. It also sits between its detachable controllers and is powered by an AMD Ryzen Z1 Extreme processor. It also has a bigger battery compared to the Steam Deck and its alternatives, but that, unfortunately, still won't be enough to keep up with long gaming sessions, especially if you're running more demanding titles. And that's exactly why a portable power bank is almost a must.

There's no shortage of power banks on the market, but you'll need something that'll keep your Legion Go handheld charged for several hours when you're away from a power outlet. You'll also need something that can deliver more than 65W of power to ensure it's getting topped up at the right speeds. With that in mind, I've highlighted some of the best Legion Go power banks below for you to check out:

  • Anker 747 Power Bank
    Editor's choice
    $150 at Amazon
  • Source: Baseus
    Baseus 20,000mAh 65W power bank
    Best value
    $60 at Amazon
  • Anker 737 PowerCore
    Premium pick
    $160 at Anker
  • Source: Anker
    Anker 733 Power Bank
    2-in-1 hybrid charger
    $100 at Amazon
  • Source: Baseus
    Baseus 100W GaN II USB-C Charger
    Sleek design

item.description:
The upcoming Lenovo Legion Go is a pretty exciting device. This Windows-powered handheld gaming device will let you run most PC games on a huge and bright 8.8-inch QHD+ screen. It also sits between its detachable controllers and is powered by an AMD Ryzen Z1 Extreme processor. It also has a bigger battery compared to the Steam Deck and its alternatives, but that, unfortunately, still won't be enough to keep up with long gaming sessions, especially if you're running more demanding titles. And that's exactly why a portable power bank is almost a must.

There's no shortage of power banks on the market, but you'll need something that'll keep your Legion Go handheld charged for several hours when you're away from a power outlet. You'll also need something that can deliver more than 65W of power to ensure it's getting topped up at the right speeds. With that in mind, I've highlighted some of the best Legion Go power banks below for you to check out:

  • Anker 747 Power Bank
    Editor's choice
    $150 at Amazon
  • Source: Baseus

item.title: iPhone 15: Dynamic Island, USB-C, A16 Bionic, and everything you need to know
item.itunes:title: iPhone 15: Dynamic Island, USB-C, A16 Bionic, and everything you need to know
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • iPhone 15: Release date
  • Display: The iPhone 15 could be your next holiday destination
  • Ports: C-ing is believing
  • Charging: Will Apple reverse its standards?
  • Processor: The iPhone 15 belongs in 2022
  • Cameras: It ain't a Pro, bro

The iPhone 14 and iPhone 14 Pro will soon no longer be the latest iPhones, as Apple is preparing to launch a fresh batch of flagship phones. For years now, the Cupertino firm has been refreshing its premium line of smartphones every fall, and this year will likely be no different. So what will the iPhone 15 and iPhone 15 Plus introduce, and what do we want Apple to include in them? Let's unpack!

iPhone 15: Release date

Apple is holding a special event on Sept. 12 at 10:00 a.m. PT. That's likely when we'll get our first official look at the iPhone 15 and iPhone 15 Plus. Customers will likely be able to preorder them later that week, with shipping potentially following a week later. This time around, the company could be offering the devices in Blue, Yellow, Pink, White, and Black colorways.

Display: The iPhone 15 could be your next holiday destination

Let's start with some of the exciting changes we could see in the regular 2023 models. Is the Dynamic Island one of your bucket-list resorts, but you just can't justify paying for a Pro iPhone? Wish no more! We expect this hardware/software sorcery to make a debut on the regular iPhone 15 and 15 Plus models. This means in 2023, you might not need to invest in a Pro model to rid yourself of the infamous notch. However, the always-on display might remain Pro-exclusive.

Source: 9to5Mac

In the display department, we also expect Apple to retain the 6.1-inch and 6.7-inch variants or slightly expand them by around 0.1 inch. Apart from a larger screen and battery, we presume that the Plus model will pack the same specs as the 6.1-inch regular model. Meanwhile, in the refresh rate field, there are seemingly no relevant updates. The regular iPhone 15 and 15 Plus will likely stick to the standard 60Hz and miss out on the ProMotion 120Hz technology.

Ports: C-ing is believing

Apple has been depending on the Lightning port for... we've lost count. This decade-old technology might finally be disappearing with the 2023 iPhone release. We've been hearing rumors about Apple finally adopting the USB Type-C charging port on the upcoming line — as hard as it is to believe. This change is likely due to EU regulations that pushed the company to adopt the more universal charger and drop its proprietary one.

Also, in the ports department, the headphone jack is finally making a return... not. Sorry. Apple dropped the physical SIM tray for U.S. iPhone 14 models. We expect the iPhone 15 and 15 Plus to receive the same treatment, at least in the United States. The company could expand this to even more regions, though. Is the rest of the world ready for an eSIM-only execution? Likely not. However, the company could analyze and roll out these hardware changes throughout the upcoming years as it sees fit. Carriers worldwide are probably already in alert mode following the eSIM-only iPhone launch in the U.S. Whether they take Apple's move seriously and start supporting the technology (where it isn't already available) is yet to be seen.

Charging: Will Apple reverse its standards?

For a few years now, Apple has supported three charging standards on its iPhones. You get to use a Lightning cable for wired charging. Alternatively, you can use any Qi pad for slow wireless charging. Lastly, you get the MagSafe protocol for faster wireless charging speeds. However, one main charging-related feature remains absent on all iPhones Apple products — reverse wireless charging.

For the unfamiliar, some companies like Samsung allow users to turn their phones into Qi pads. This way, users get to charge their earbuds or other accessories through their phones. Right now, there is seemingly no news regarding the iPhone 15 supporting this feature. However, it certainly has a top spot on our wishlist. A man can dream.

Putting our dreams aside, the iPhone 15 and iPhone 15 Plus could support the Qi2 standard, which is similar to MagSafe. If this comes to fruition, then these phones could take advantage of faster wireless charging speeds without needing to rely on MagSafe accessories. After all, Qi2, in a way, replicates the MagSafe concept by including alignment magnets that keep the device attached to a charger or accessory firmly.

Processor: The iPhone 15 belongs in 2022

With the release of the iPhone 14 series, Apple started using a year-old processor on the regular models. This further defines what a Pro iPhone is and can do. Rumors lead us to believe that the Cupertino overlord will be adopting this strategy for the foreseeable future. This would mean the regular iPhone 15 and 15 Plus get 2022's A16 Bionic chip. Meanwhile, the iPhone 15 Pro models will likely get an exclusive taste of the upcoming A17 Bionic chipset.

And speaking of chipsets, the ultra-wideband (U1) chip first introduced in the iPhone 11 could be getting an upgrade when the iPhone 15 launches. Apple is reportedly switching the production process from 16nm to 7nm to boost its performance and make it more energy efficient. This would also presumably boost the iPhone's connection to the Vision Pro.

Cameras: It ain't a Pro, bro

Considering we're discussing the regular iPhone 15 models, you can't expect them to receive the best camera treatment from Apple. For years, one of the most obvious and prominent distinguishing factors between regular and Pro iPhones has been the rear camera system. While the highest-end Pro iPhone could gain a Periscope lens in 2023, we're almost certain the regular ones won't. You likely will get the usual dual-lens layout on the back with some new gimmicky features, such as Action Mode on the iPhone 14.

Notably, though, Apple could depend on Sony's newest image sensors, which would double the saturation in each pixel. The end result would be more accurate shots that cut down on under and over-saturation. We've also been reading rumors about the company potentially bumping the wide camera's resolution from 12MP to 48MP. This upgrade was first applied to the iPhone 14 Pro and iPhone 14 Pro Max in 2022.

Bottom Line: Is the regular iPhone becoming a higher-end SE?

It's no secret at this point that Apple is aggressively trying to sell more Pro iPhone units. With every passing year, the Cupertino tech giant is increasing the gap between the regular and highest-end models. This begs the question — are regular "high-end" iPhones slowly becoming just higher-tier SE models?

SE iPhones include a mix of outdated and new tech for a budget-friendly price. Regular iPhones are slowly starting to include more and more of what's old and less of the exciting, new offerings — which remain Pro-exclusive. This, in a way, makes them "middle-range" phones in the company's context with matching price tags. Apple could potentially introduce a redesigned iPhone chassis in 2023. However, if that's the case, will the regular iPhone 15 and 15 Plus even get it? Considering Apple's new strategy, likely not.
